mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces borrowing costs for corporations and households, directly boosting equity valuations through lower discount rates and higher expected cash flows over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, it signals severe labor market slack and weak consumer spending power, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series rise, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher short-term equity returns as markets price in improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or zero correlation with INDPRO momentum, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate indicate real interest rate compression and inflation risk, prompting equity sell-offs as investors demand higher risk premiums over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0 while FEDFUNDS stays flat, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 over three months confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the payroll growth threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of real gross domestic product reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate earnings expectations and sustains positive equity market trends over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 levels drops below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95.0 while unemployment remains elevated, it signals a supply-demand mismatch and economic stagnation, leading to negative equity returns as markets discount future earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO is below 95.0 and UNRATE is high, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over baseline models.
